title: Most Frequent Element
description:
Given an array of integers `nums`, find the element that appears most frequently. If multiple elements have the same highest frequency, return the **smallest** among them.

**Constraints:**
- 1 <= nums.length <= 10^5
- -10^4 <= nums[i] <= 10^4
hints: Use a hash map (object/dictionary) to count how many times each number appears.
As you count, keep track of the element with the highest frequency seen so far.
When two elements have the same frequency, keep the smaller one.

complexity.explanation: Single pass through the array; hash map stores at most n unique entries.
